title: BUbuisnessAndMarket
description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from BUbuisnessAndMarket, skipping any feeds with errors.

items.title: Wendy’s closes hundreds of restaurants after plunge in same-store sales worse than Wall Street expected
items.description: "One learning from 2025 around value, we swung the pendulum too far towards limited-time price promotions instead of everyday value."

items.title: Amazon’s Ring decides maybe partnering with a police surveillance firm is a bad idea after wide revulsion at Super Bowl ad
items.description: The Super Bowl ad depicts a lost dog being tracked by cameras throughout a neighborhood using artificial intelligence.

items.title: Americans’ new tariff coffee math means ditching the Starbucks, McDonald’s and Dunkin’ runs
items.description: "Twenty cents a cup compared to $7 or $8 a cup. The math just makes sense."

items.title: Top Hollywood screenwriter warns TikTok’s new tool is at the gates: ‘I hate to say it. It’s likely over for us’
items.description: Seedance 2.0, which is only available in China for now, lets users generate high-quality AI videos using simple text prompts.

items.title: The ultrawealthy have 3 big secrets on reducing taxes including the way they die
items.description: “Assets like stocks can quietly grow for decades and, when they’re inherited, the tax bill often disappears.”
